core_mechanism: a finite-wavevector (roton) instability of a homogeneous field driven by a nonlocal interaction kernel whose momentum-space transform has a partly negative (attractive) component, so that the excitation dispersion develops a local minimum that softens toward zero and triggers spontaneous periodic pattern formation / symmetry-breaking crystallization
brief_reason: The abstract states that effective nonlocal interactions that are isotropically repulsive in real space nonetheless produce partial attraction in momentum space, yielding a roton-maxon excitation spectrum whose softening drives a transition to a spatially modulated (supersolid) state.
